data.title: Deere GAAP EPS of $3.19 misses by $0.06, revenue of $8.51B beats by $620M
data.description:
• Deere press release (NYSE:DE): Q4 GAAP EPS of $3.19 misses by $0.06.
  • Revenue of $8.51B (-30.2% Y/Y) beats by $620M.
  • Company Outlook & Summary
  • Net income attributable to Deere & Company for fiscal 2025 is forecasted to remain in a range of $5.0 billion to $5.5 billion.

data.title: Iron Mountain to take minority stake in Ooredoo’s data centre company MENA Digital Hub
data.description:
Information management services company Iron Mountain (NYSE:IRM) to take a minority equity stake in Ooredoo’s carrier-neutral data centre company, MENA Digital Hub, it said on Thursday.

Iron Mountain (NYSE:IRM) will provide MENA Digital Hub with specialised advice in the design, construction and daily operations of data centres to meet evolving global demands.

Initial engagement will focus on operational support, infrastructure enhancement and supporting MENA Digital Hub’s existing plans to further expand its data centre capacity across key markets in the MENA region, the companies said.

data.title: Walker & Dunlop Non-GAAP EPS of $1.34 misses by $0.17, revenue of $341.5M beats by $28.39M
data.description:
• Walker & Dunlop press release (NYSE:WD): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.34 misses by $0.17.
  • Revenue of $341.5M (+24.5% Y/Y) beats by $28.39M.
  • Total transaction volume of $13.4 billion, up 45% from Q4’23. Total transaction volumes increased 45% in the fourth quarter of 2024, reflecting the relative strength and continued demand throughout the multifamily market despite the continued volatility of interest rates during the fourth quarter of 2024.

data.title: JPMorgan's Jamie Dimon slams employee petition on hybrid work - report
data.description:
JPMorgan Chase (NYSE:JPM) CEO Jamie Dimon has derided a petition by some employees for the bank to roll back its five-day return-to-office policy, Reuters reported, citing a recording of a town hall meeting on Wednesday.

Employees have complained about losing hybrid-work arrangements on internal message boards, leading to an online petition calling on Dimon to reconsider the five-day policy. The petition received about 950 signatures by Wednesday evening.

"Don't waste time on it," Dimon responded when asked about the petition during the town hall meeting. "I don't care how many people sign that f***ing petition."

The CEO noted that some employees did not pay attention during Zoom meetings, which affected their efficiency. He asked all departments to show 10% gains in efficiency, which is said to entail 10% cuts in reports, meetings, and training sessions.

As for leaving in-office requirements to managers, Dimon declared: "Zero chance. The abuse that took place is extraordinary."

Dimon's views echo those of U.S. President Donald Trump, who is working to end remote work arrangements in the federal government.

JPMorgan (NYSE:JPM) last month told its employees that they must return to the office five days a week starting early next month. About 40% of the bank's employees currently work on a hybrid schedule.

According to employees petitioning to reverse this move, the staff's work "continuously produced record profits for the bank - all while working from home and from the office in a hybrid manner."

The petition comes as some JPMorgan (JPM) employees have reached out to the Communications Workers of America on how to unionize. The CWA recently helped Wells Fargo (WFC) employees in an Albuquerque branch to unionize - a first for a major U.S. bank.

data.title: Brunswick names CEO David Foulkes as chair
data.description:
• Brunswick (NYSE:BC) said it has named CEO David Foulkes as chair of the board, effective March 1.
  • The firm added non-executive board chair Nancy Cooper will remain on the Brunswick board, and current board member David Everitt will take on the role of lead independent director.
  • Foulkes was appointed the CEO in January 2019.

data.title: British American Tobacco reports FY results
data.description:
• British American Tobacco press release (NYSE:BTI): FY GAAP EPS of 136.0 p
  • Revenue of £25.87B (-5.2% Y/Y).
  • Adj. gross margin of 67.2% up 60bps
  • Total adjusted organic gross profit at constant rates, grew by £396 million, an increase of 2.2% compared to 2023
  • 2025 Outlook – Global tobacco industry volume expected to be down c.2%. – c.1% revenue growth (at constant rates), as we navigate increased excise and VAT in Bangladesh and new tobacco regulations in Australia. – 1.5-2.5% adjusted profit from operations growth (adjusted for Canada, at constant rates)*, including an expected c.1.5% transactional FX headwind. – Performance expected to be second half weighted as we deploy our innovations throughout the year. – Net finance costs* expected to be around £1.8 billion (adjusted for Canada).

data.title: Himax Technologies beats Q4 top-line and bottom-line estimates; initiates Q1 outlook
data.description:
• Himax Technologies press release (NASDAQ:HIMX): Q4 GAAP EPADS of $0.14 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $237.2M (+4.2% Y/Y) beats by $16.11M.
  • Himax had $224.6 million of cash, cash equivalents and other financial assets as of December 31, 2024. This compares to $206.4 million at the same time last year and $206.5 million a quarter ago.
  • Himax achieved a strong positive operating cash flow of $35.4 million for the fourth quarter, compared to a cash outflow of $3.1 million in Q3.
  • Total capital expenditures for 2024 were $13.1 million as compared to $23.4 million in 2023.
  • First Quarter 2025 Guidance
    Net Revenue: Decrease 8.5% to 12.5% QoQ, Flat to Up 4.6% YoY vs. consensus of $200.07M.
    Gross Margin: Around 30.5%, depending on final product mix
    Profit: 9.0 cents to 11.0 cents per diluted ADS, Up 26% to 54% YoY vs. consensus of $0.08.
